title: Alefiya Hussain
text:
I’m a researcher at the USC Information Sciences Institute (USC-ISI) with more than three decades of experience building and operating cutting-edge platforms for networked cybersecurity experimentation, data sharing, and reproducible research. I have served as PI or co-PI on multiple DARPA, NSF, and DHS programs.
I lead or co-lead DeterLab — for over 19 years the only public research testbed for cybersecurity, serving 1,000+ researchers across 278 institutions and 46 countries — and the Merge control-software ecosystem that powers independent testbeds for DARPA and NSF. My work spans experimentation infrastructure, measurements and benchmarks, large-scale data collection for machine learning, and the methodology that makes networking experiments repeatable and reproducible.
I also lead extensive testing & evaluation (T&E) for DARPA programs — most recently partnering with Sandia National Laboratories to evaluate three technologies in the DARPA SearchLight program, producing an 800+ page final assessment.
On this site you’ll find my research, publications, a technical blog, and my CV.





Experimentation Infrastructure | Testbeds and platforms for networking & cybersecurity research — DeterLab, MergeTB, MAGI, ThirdEye  Measurements, Metrics & Benchmarks | Rigorous, reproducible measurement of denial-of-service and defense effectiveness  Reproducibility & Repeatability | Methodology and fidelity studies for trustworthy networking experiments  Cyber-Physical Systems | Statistical modeling of interconnected techno-economic-social systems (e.g. smart grids)  Data for Machine Learning | Large-scale, representative data-collection efforts to enable ML research

title: Research
text: My research builds the infrastructure, methods, and metrics that make cybersecurity and networking experimentation rigorous, reproducible, and useful to the broader community. Below are the main threads, along with the platforms my team has designed and operated.

title: Research
text:
Research Threads

Cybersecurity Experimentation Infrastructure
Developing and maintaining networked cybersecurity experimentation and data-sharing platforms — from semantic frameworks for analyzing experiment data to orchestration of complex, multi-domain experiments.


Measurements, Metrics & Benchmarks
Developing robust measurements and user-centric metrics with the goal of creating rigorous, community-adopted benchmarks — including accurate measurement of denial-of-service impact and defense effectiveness.


Reproducibility & Repeatability
Experimentation methodology for repeatability and reproducibility in networking research, including replicated testbed studies and fidelity analyses of traffic generation and network emulation.


Cyber-Physical Systems & Cross-Disciplinary Modeling
Statistical modeling of complex, interconnected techno-economic-social systems — for example, security and resiliency of energy cyber-physical systems and strategic adversaries in dynamic-pricing markets.


Data Collection for Machine Learning
Leading large-scale, representative data-collection efforts (such as the DARPA SearchLight application-traffic dataset) to enable machine-learning research on realistic network behavior.


Community, Tutorials & Workshops
Cultivating research communities and disseminating best practices through tutorials, workshops, and shared testbed resources.

title: Research
text:
Infrastructure I Build & Operate

DeterLab
For 19+ years, the only public research infrastructure dedicated to cybersecurity (NSF, DARPA, and DHS funded). To date it has served 389 research projects, 278 institutions, 1,042 researchers across 205 locations in 46 countries.


Merge (MergeTB)
Control software for experimental environments, deployed to run three independent infrastructures: DARPA DCOMP, DARPA SearchLight, and NSF DeterLab.


Experimentation Tools



Tool
Purpose




LegoTG / Flooder
Realistic traffic generation


VTC generator
Representative video-teleconference traffic


Network emulator
High-fidelity network emulation


FARM (dash)
Human user-behavior models


MAGI
Experiment automation


ThirdEye
Experiment logging & monitoring


DDoS benchmarks
Benchmarking suite

title: Research
text:
Testing & Evaluation
USC-ISI has extensive experience in testing & evaluation (T&E) for DARPA programs. Evaluations are structured as claims and hypotheses aligned with solicitation-defined metrics, exercised across a wide range of scenarios to surface both strengths and limitations, with iterative assessments delivered to the funding agency. Most recently, my team partnered with Sandia National Laboratories to lead the evaluation of three technologies in the DARPA SearchLight program, delivering an 800+ page final assessment.

title: Research
text:
Secure & Classified Facilities
USC-ISI operates a dedicated IT team and infrastructure supporting export-controlled and classified work, including a NIST SP 800-171 enclave, SCIFs, and DOD-accredited secure spaces and IT systems in Los Angeles, CA and Arlington, VA.

title: Welcome
text:
Welcome to my blog. This is where I’ll write about the things my research touches day to day: building and operating cybersecurity testbeds, measuring denial-of-service and defenses in ways that actually reproduce, generating realistic network traffic, and the practical side of making experiments trustworthy.
Expect posts that pair a research paper with the why it matters and, where useful, pointers to code and data. If you’re new here, start with my research or browse the publications.
More soon.
